$schema: DoveDesign interaction state machine — see CLAUDE.md '# CAD Architecture & Rules'
description: Global interaction states for the CAD viewport. Tools transition through these states in response to input events. No tool may mutate board/annotation state outside of the transitions defined here reaching a terminal COMMIT action, which must go through the single recalculation pipeline (DoveDesignEngine.updateWorkspaceState per CAD_ENGINE_BLUEPRINT.ts).

IDLE.description: No tool action in progress. Camera orbit/pan/zoom is live. User may click to select, or press a tool hotkey to arm a tool.

SELECT_FACE.description: User has picked a Face on a Solid (raycast hit resolved to a faceId, not a raw world point). This is a read-only selection state — no geometry is created or mutated yet.

DRAW_STUD_START.description: Draw tool armed, waiting for the first click to place the board's start point. Orbit controls must be disabled once the drag actually begins, not on tool arm.

DRAW_STUD_END.description: Start point captured (as parameters, not a cached world coordinate). Waiting for drag/second click to determine length, then commit. On commit, a new SolidBoard is created via BoardParameters only — never a hand-authored mesh.

notes: POINTER_UP_BELOW_THRESHOLD cancels the draw without committing a board (guards against accidental single-click boards).
Every transition into IDLE from a drawing/dragging state must restore orbitControlsEnabled = true.
This file defines states only. It does not itself run any geometry math — see CAD_ENGINE_BLUEPRINT.ts for the pure evaluator pipeline that a COMMIT transition must call into.
